title: tanacetum hololeucum
intro: Tanacetum hololeucum, also known as white pyrethrum, is a flowering plant in the daisy family native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone compounds, particularly hololeucin. The health benefits of Tanacetum hololeucum include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as acne, eczema, and wounds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Tanacetum hololeucum, including tea, tincture, and essential oil, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
